mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount-rate pressure on equities and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation does not accelerate, because unchanged CPIAUCSL avoids an offsetting tightening expectation.
regime_hint: late-cycle disinflation or pause in tightening
failure_condition: If 20d SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with flat-or-falling FEDFUNDS and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 3-year window, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower equity earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following week. The effect is fast because markets reprice growth and recession risk before hard data confirm it.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 5d SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if PAYEMS continues rising while the signal remains negative and the hit rate falls to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand and housing-related activity, which supports cyclical earnings and lifts the S&P 500 over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when sales rise only because of price inflation while housing collapses, because then real demand is not strengthening.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS/HOUST improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-strength episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL remains elevated signals an adverse supply shock: weaker real output with sticky inflation raises recession odds and compresses equity multiples. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% in 2 months while CPIAUCSL is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if the combined slowdown-plus-sticky-inflation condition fails to produce negative 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the signal is neutral once only INDPRO is weak.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate real activity and supports corporate revenue growth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The effect is slower than market-led indicators because GDP is a lagged confirmation of underlying demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDP growth does not correspond to above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the 60d hit rate remains below 52% over any 4-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%, additional tightening pressure should be interpreted as restrictive policy that weakens risk appetite and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is specifically about high-rate regimes; the same rate level is less informative when policy is accommodative and inflation is falling.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal loses predictive power once the policy rate is already expected and unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend combined with a rising UNRATE means job creation is slowing and unemployment is worsening, which should trigger a negative equity response over the next week. This is a joint labor-market deterioration signal and is stronger than either series alone because it captures both payroll loss and labor slack.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fail to generate negative 5d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no value beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A weakening HOUST series alongside weaker RSAFS signals a broad pullback in interest-sensitive household and retail demand, which should pressure cyclical earnings and deliver negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if housing weakens only because of supply constraints while sales remain strong.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% in 3 months while RSAFS is flat or negative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-and-sales deterioration does not produce negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the signal disappears when housing weakness is not accompanied by weak retail sales.
